name: RTB101 phase 2b signal did not replicate in phase 3 respiratory-illness trial
summary: The larger RTB101 program is the key limiting human evidence: one phase 2b analysis was positive, but the phase 3 trial missed its respiratory-illness endpoint.
statement: In adults aged 65 years and older, RTB101 showed a phase 2b respiratory-infection signal in a prespecified analysis, but the phase 3 trial did not reduce clinically symptomatic respiratory illness versus placebo.
time_horizon: Phase 2b and phase 3 respiratory-illness trial follow-up
quantitative_note: Phase 2b part 2 reported 19% with at least one laboratory-confirmed RTI on RTB101 10 mg once daily versus 28% pooled placebo (OR 0.601, 90% CI 0.391-0.922, p=0.02); phase 3 reported 26% clinically symptomatic respiratory illness on RTB101 versus 25% placebo (OR 1.07, 90% CI 0.80-1.42, p=0.65).
caveats: The positive phase 2b result did not translate into a successful phase 3 clinical endpoint.
Endpoint definitions differed between laboratory-confirmed respiratory infection and clinically symptomatic respiratory illness.
The program supports a mixed, low-confidence public read rather than a broad immune rejuvenation claim.
